Title: DSA Quick Notes (2-Page Study Guide)
Content:
DSA Quick Notes (2-Page Study Guide)
Concise reference for common data structures, algorithms, and complexities.
Arrays: Contiguous memory storage with O(1) access. Search O(n), insertion/deletion O(n). Best for
indexed lookups.
Linked Lists: Nodes connected by pointers. Flexible insertion/deletion. Access/search O(n),
insert/delete at head O(1).
Stacks: LIFO structure. Push/pop/peek O(1). Used in recursion, undo, expression evaluation, DFS.
Queues: FIFO structure. Enqueue/dequeue O(1). Used in BFS, scheduling, buffering.
Hash Tables: Key-value storage using hash functions. Average insert/search/delete O(1), worst O(n)
with collisions.
Trees: Hierarchical structure. BST supports average O(log n) search/insert/delete; worst O(n) if
unbalanced.
Graphs: Nodes and edges. Used to model networks. Traversal via BFS/DFS in O(V+E).
Binary Search: Searches sorted arrays by halving search space. Time O(log n), space O(1).
BFS: Breadth-first traversal using queue. Visits level by level. Useful for shortest path in unweighted
graphs.
DFS: Depth-first traversal using recursion or stack. Useful for backtracking and path exploration.
